title: Space4Cities: dynamic management of urban public space
user: Space4Cities consortium (coordinator Forum Virium Helsinki; cities incl. Amsterdam, Guimarães, Attica Region)
location: Helsinki, Amsterdam, Guimarães, Attica & more (Horizon Europe / EUSPA)
summary: The EUSPA-funded Space4Cities project builds replicable, space-data-based solutions for dynamic management of urban public spaces, on the ground and in the air.
challenge: Public-space management lags behind other smart-city domains in digital transformation. Cities cannot manage public space — on ground and in air — in an agile way that adapts to fast-changing needs, events and climate disruptions.
approach: Space4Cities (Horizon Europe, EUSPA, grant 101131955; €5.2M, coordinated by Forum Virium Helsinki) exploits Copernicus and Galileo data to build replicable solutions for dynamic, data-driven management of public areas, with a roadmap for Europe-wide implementation. City partners including Amsterdam, Guimarães and the Attica Region help define and validate the needs.
results: The project targets agile reallocation of public space, better response to disruptive events and increased urban resilience — demonstrating how space data can move public-space management from static planning to dynamic, evidence-based operation.
